content.content:
1‚Üí# CLAUDE.md
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí## Project Overview
     6‚Üí
     7‚ÜíMaimai Semi-Automated Recruitment Assistant - A Python CLI tool for semi-automating candidate search, profile extraction, and outreach on Maimai (ËÑâËÑâ), China's professional networking platform. Core modules (browser, auth, search, profile, message, export) are implemented and functional.
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí## Build & Development Commands
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí```bash
    12‚Üí# Install dependencies
    13‚Üípip install -r requirements.txt
    14‚Üípatchright install chrome   # Uses real Chrome for better anti-detection
    15‚Üí# Or: patchright install chromium
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí# Initialize database (creates SQLite tables)
    18‚Üípython scripts/setup_db.py
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí# Start Chrome with remote debugging (required before running CLI)
    21‚Üí# Chrome 144+ requires --user-data-dir to avoid conflicts with existing Chrome instances
    22‚Üí/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222 --user-data-dir=/tmp/chrome-debug-profile
    23‚Üí# Then log in to Maimai manually in that Chrome window
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí# Run CLI (connects to Chrome via CDP)
    26‚Üípython src/cli/main.py --help
    27‚Üípython src/cli/main.py login                    # Verify login status via CDP
    28‚Üípython src/cli/main.py search -k "ÂâçÁ´ØÂ∑•Á®ãÂ∏à" -p 2  # Search candidates
    29‚Üípython src/cli/main.py search -k "ÂâçÁ´Ø" -k "ÂêéÁ´Ø" -p 2  # Multi-keyword search
    30‚Üípython src/cli/main.py search -k "ÂâçÁ´Ø" -c "Â≠óËäÇ" -c "Âø´Êâã" -p 2  # Multi-company filter
    31‚Üípython src/cli/main.py profile <maimai_id>       # View profile
    32‚Üípython src/cli/main.py message <id> -t greeting  # Send message
    33‚Üípython src/cli/main.py export -o out.xlsx        # Export data
    34‚Üípython src/cli/main.py pipeline -k "ÂâçÁ´Ø" -t greeting -v "recruiter_name=ÂºÝ‰∏â" -p 2  # Search + batch message
    35‚Üípython src/cli/main.py pipeline -k "ÂâçÁ´Ø" -k "ÂêéÁ´Ø" -t greeting --dry-run  # Pipeline preview only
    36‚Üípython src/cli/main.py pipeline -k "ÁÝîÂèë" -c "‰∫öÈ©¨ÈÄä" -c "Âø´Êâã" -t greeting -p 3  # Multi-company filter
    37‚Üípython src/cli/main.py pipeline -k "ÂâçÁ´Ø" -t greeting -p 10 -b 2  # Batch: search 2 pages, send, repeat
    38‚Üípython src/cli/main.py pipeline --resume                         # Resume interrupted pipeline
    39‚Üípython src/cli/main.py templates                 # List templates
    40‚Üípython src/cli/main.py status                    # Rate limit status
    41‚Üípython src/cli/main.py logout                    # Clear session backup
    42‚Üípython src/cli/main.py --cdp http://localhost:9333 search -k "ÂâçÁ´Ø" -p 1  # Custom CDP port
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí# Testing
    45‚Üípytest                      # Run all tests
    46‚Üípytest --cov=src tests/    # With coverage
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí# Code quality
    49‚Üíblack src/ tests/          # Format code
    50‚Üíruff check src/ tests/     # Lint
    51‚Üímypy src/                  # Type check
    52‚Üí```
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí## Architecture
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí```
    57‚ÜíCLI Layer (Click)           ‚Üí  src/cli/main.py (10 commands: login, search, profile, message, pipeline, export, templates, status, logout)
    58‚Üí       ‚Üì
    59‚ÜíFeature Modules             ‚Üí  src/modules/ (auth, search, profile, message, export)
    60‚Üí       ‚Üì
    61‚ÜíCore Infrastructure         ‚Üí  src/core/ (browser, session, anti_detection)
    62‚Üí       ‚Üì
    63‚Üí‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¥‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
    64‚ÜíDatabase      Config       Utilities
    65‚Üí(SQLAlchemy)  (3 JSON)     (logging, validation, rate limiting)
    66‚Üí```
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí**Core infrastructure** (in `src/core/`):
    69‚Üí- `browser.py` - Singleton BrowserManager connecting to user's Chrome via CDP (`connect_over_cdp`), reuses existing context/page, lazy asyncio.Lock. Disconnect-only close (Chrome stays open).
    70‚Üí- `session.py` - SessionManager for save/load cookies via Patchright storage_state
    71‚Üí- `anti_detection.py` - Multi-layer anti-detection: Patchright (undetectable automation), Emunium (human-like click/type), Ghost Cursor algorithm (Fitts's Law + cubic Bezier mouse trajectories with sigmoid easing and micro-overshoot), stealth JS, Chrome 131-136 UA strings
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí**Feature modules** (in `src/modules/`):
    74‚Üí- `auth.py` - CDP login verification (user logs in manually in Chrome), captcha detection/pause
    75‚Üí- `search.py` - Candidate search with JS-based extraction (name+company+title hash IDs, location, profile URL), multi-keyword support, multi-company filtering, pagination, captcha detection with adaptive throttle reporting
    76‚Üí- `profile.py` - Profile extraction (education, work experience, skills), async rate limiting
    77‚Üí- `message.py` - Template rendering with variable substitution, questionary-based confirmation (unsafe_ask for async compatibility), batch sending with single rate-limit check, auto-creates Candidate records for FK integrity, message send verification, existing conversation detection
    78‚Üí- `export.py` - Dual-format export (Excel with openpyxl formatting / CSV)
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí**Utilities** (in `src/utils/`):
    81‚Üí- `config.py` - Singleton config loader with dot-notation access (e.g., `config.get('browser.headless')`)
    82‚Üí- `rate_limiter.py` - Database-backed rate limiting with configurable limits from `settings.json`, adaptive throttling (auto-reduces limits on captcha/error detection)
    83‚Üí- `delays.py` - Human-like delays using log-normal distribution, fatigue simulation (delays increase over session), random distraction pauses, time-of-day awareness
    84‚Üí- `retry.py` - Navigation retry with tenacity (3 attempts, exponential backoff) for `page.goto()` calls
    85‚Üí- `checkpoint.py` - Pipeline checkpoint save/load for resume capability (`--resume` flag)
    86‚Üí- `scoring.py` - Candidate scoring by title seniority, company tier, and keyword relevance for priority-based messaging
    87‚Üí- `validators.py` - Input validation for IDs, keywords, URLs, file paths
    88‚Üí- `logger.py` - Loguru-based logging with rotation
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí**Database** (`src/models/database.py`): SQLAlchemy ORM with 4 tables - Candidates, Messages, SearchHistory, ActionLogs. `DatabaseManager.session_scope()` context manager for safe session handling (auto commit/rollback/close).
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí## Configuration Files
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí- `config/settings.json` - Runtime settings (browser, delays, paths)
    95‚Üí- `config/selectors.json` - CSS selectors for Maimai pages (**PLACEHOLDERS - must be updated with actual selectors**)
    96‚Üí- `config/message_templates.json` - 5 message templates with variable substitution
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí## Key Constraints
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí- **CDP connection mode** - tool connects to user's Chrome via `--remote-debugging-port=9222`; user manages Chrome lifecycle and login; `close()` disconnects without killing Chrome
   101‚Üí- **Rate limits are configurable** via `config/settings.json` with adaptive throttling (auto-reduces on captcha/error)
   102‚Üí- **Selectors updated** - JS-based extraction uses avatar image detection rather than CSS selectors; `selectors.json` has real Maimai selectors but primary extraction is via `page.evaluate()`
   103‚Üí- **Python 3.10+** required
   104‚Üí- All message operations require human confirmation
   105‚Üí- **Captcha handling** - search and login flows auto-detect captcha elements and pause for manual resolution
   106‚Üí- **Navigation retries** - all `page.goto()` calls go through `retry_navigation()` (tenacity, 3 attempts, exponential backoff)
   107‚Üí- **DB sessions** - use `db_manager.session_scope()` context manager for all DB operations (auto commit/rollback/close); avoid manual `get_session()` / `commit()` / `close()` patterns
   108‚Üí- **asyncio** - `run_async()` uses `asyncio.run()`; within async code use `asyncio.get_running_loop()` (never `get_event_loop()`)
   109‚Üí- **Search IDs** - prefer real user IDs from `contactcard_{user_id}` DOM elements (format: `mm_{user_id}`), falling back to `hash(name + company + title)`; DB dedup also checks name+company
   110‚Üí

    50‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ conftest.py               # ‚úì Pytest fixtures
    51‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test_config.py            # ‚úì Configuration tests
    52‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ test_validators.py        # ‚úì Validation tests
    53‚Üí‚îÇ
    54‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ scripts/                       # Utility scripts
    55‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ setup_db.py               # ‚úì Database initialization
    56‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ backup_data.py            # ‚úì Database backup
    57‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ update_selectors.py       # TODO: Selector update helper
    58‚Üí‚îÇ
    59‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .gitignore                     # ‚úì Git ignore rules
    60‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ LICENSE                        # ‚úì MIT License

    64‚Üí‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ pyproject.toml                 # ‚úì Project configuration
    65‚Üí```
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí## Implementation Status
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí### ‚úÖ Completed (Phase 1 - Foundation)
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí1. **Project Structure**
    72‚Üí   - All directories created
    73‚Üí   - Configuration files in place
    74‚Üí   - Git repository initialized
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí2. **Core Utilities**
    77‚Üí   - `src/utils/config.py` - Configuration loader
    78‚Üí   - `src/utils/logger.py` - Logging setup
    79‚Üí   - `src/utils/delays.py` - Random delay generator
    80‚Üí   - `src/utils/validators.py` - Input validation
    81‚Üí   - `src/utils/rate_limiter.py` - Rate limiting
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí3. **Data Layer**
    84‚Üí   - `src/models/database.py` - SQLAlchemy models
    85‚Üí   - Database schema defined (Candidate, Message, SearchHistory, ActionLog)
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí4. **CLI Interface**
    88‚Üí   - `src/cli/main.py` - Basic CLI structure
    89‚Üí   - Command placeholders (login, search, profile, message, export)
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí5. **Exception Handling**
    92‚Üí   - `src/exceptions/errors.py` - Custom exceptions
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí6. **Configuration**
    95‚Üí   - `config/settings.json` - Rate limits, delays, paths
    96‚Üí   - `config/selectors.json` - Page selectors (placeholders)
    97‚Üí   - `config/message_templates.json` - 5 message templates
    98‚Üí
    99‚Üí7. **Testing**
   100‚Üí   - `tests/conftest.py` - Pytest fixtures
   101‚Üí   - `tests/test_config.py` - Configuration tests
   102‚Üí   - `tests/test_validators.py` - Validation tests
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí8. **Scripts**
   105‚Üí   - `scripts/setup_db.py` - Database initialization
   106‚Üí   - `scripts/backup_data.py` - Database backup
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí### üöß TODO (Phase 2-5)
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí1. **Browser Automation** (Phase 1-2)
   111‚Üí   - [ ] `src/core/browser.py` - Playwright browser manager
   112‚Üí   - [ ] `src/core/anti_detection.py` - Stealth mode scripts
   113‚Üí   - [ ] `src/core/session.py` - Session persistence
   114‚Üí   - [ ] `src/modules/auth.py` - Login flow
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí2. **Core Features** (Phase 2)
   117‚Üí   - [ ] `src/modules/search.py` - Search automation
   118‚Üí   - [ ] `src/modules/profile.py` - Profile extraction
   119‚Üí   - [ ] Update `config/selectors.json` with actual Maimai selectors
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üí3. **Messaging** (Phase 3)
   122‚Üí   - [ ] `src/modules/message.py` - Message sending with confirmation
   123‚Üí   - [ ] Template variable substitution
   124‚Üí   - [ ] Preview display with Rich
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí4. **Data Export** (Phase 4)
   127‚Üí   - [ ] `src/modules/export.py` - Excel/CSV export
   128‚Üí   - [ ] Data flattening for nested JSON
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí5. **CLI Commands** (Phase 4)
   131‚Üí   - [ ] `src/cli/commands/search.py`
   132‚Üí   - [ ] `src/cli/commands/profile.py`
   133‚Üí   - [ ] `src/cli/commands/message.py`
   134‚Üí   - [ ] `src/cli/commands/export.py`
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí6. **Testing** (Phase 5)
   137‚Üí   - [ ] Unit tests for all modules
   138‚Üí   - [ ] Integration tests
   139‚Üí   - [ ] Error scenario tests
   140‚Üí
   141‚Üí## Database Schema
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí### Tables
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí1. **candidates** - Candidate profile information
   146‚Üí   - Primary key: `id` (auto-increment)
   147‚Üí   - Unique key: `maimai_id`
   148‚Üí   - JSON fields: `basic_info`, `education`, `work_experience`, `skills`
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí2. **messages** - Sent messages log
   151‚Üí   - Primary key: `id`
   152‚Üí   - Foreign key: `candidate_maimai_id` ‚Üí `candidates.maimai_id`
   153‚Üí   - Status: 'sent', 'failed', 'cancelled'
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí3. **search_history** - Search operations log
   156‚Üí   - Primary key: `id`
   157‚Üí   - JSON field: `criteria`
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí4. **action_logs** - All actions for rate limiting
   160‚Üí   - Primary key: `id`
   161‚Üí   - Indexed: `(action_type, timestamp)`
   162‚Üí   - Action types: 'search', 'view_profile', 'send_message'
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí## Configuration Files
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí### settings.json
   167‚Üí
   168‚ÜíControls application behavior:
   169‚Üí- Browser settings (headless, timeout, viewport)
   170‚Üí- Rate limits (per hour/day for each action)
   171‚Üí- Random delays (page load, actions, typing)
   172‚Üí- File paths (database, logs, exports)
   173‚Üí- Logging configuration
   174‚Üí
   175‚Üí### selectors.json
   176‚Üí
   177‚Üí‚öÝÔ∏è **CRITICAL**: Current selectors are placeholders!
   178‚Üí
   179‚ÜíBefore implementation:
   180‚Üí1. Use Chrome DevTools on actual Maimai website
   181‚Üí2. Identify stable selectors (prefer `data-*` attributes)
   182‚Üí3. Update this file with real selectors
   183‚Üí4. Test in Playwright REPL
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üí### message_templates.json
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí5 pre-configured templates:
   188‚Üí- `greeting` - Initial contact
   189‚Üí- `follow_up` - Follow-up message
   190‚Üí- `job_details` - Detailed job description
   191‚Üí- `referral_request` - Request for referrals
   192‚Üí- `thank_you` - Thank you message
   193‚Üí
   194‚ÜíEach template has:
   195‚Üí- `name` - Display name
   196‚Üí- `description` - Template purpose
   197‚Üí- `content` - Message text with variables
   198‚Üí- `variables` - Required variable list
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí## Usage Flow
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí### 1. Installation
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí```bash
   205‚Üí# Clone repository
   206‚Üícd maimai-assistant
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí# Install dependencies
   209‚Üípip install -r requirements.txt
   210‚Üíplaywright install chromium
   211‚Üí
   212‚Üí# Initialize database
   213‚Üípython scripts/setup_db.py
   214‚Üí```
   215‚Üí
   216‚Üí### 2. First-Time Setup
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí```bash
   219‚Üí# Manual login (saves session)
   220‚Üímaimai login
   221‚Üí```
   222‚Üí
   223‚Üí### 3. Daily Workflow
   224‚Üí
   225‚Üí```bash
   226‚Üí# Search candidates
   227‚Üímaimai search -k "È´òÁ∫ßÂâçÁ´ØÂ∑•Á®ãÂ∏à" -c "ÈòøÈáåÂ∑¥Â∑¥" --max-pages 3
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí# View profile
   230‚Üímaimai profile <id>
   231‚Üí
   232‚Üí# Send message (with confirmation)
   233‚Üímaimai message <id> -t greeting
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí# Export data
   236‚Üímaimai export -o results.xlsx
   237‚Üí```
   238‚Üí
   239‚Üí## Development Guidelines
   240‚Üí
   241‚Üí### Adding a New Module
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí1. Create module file in `src/modules/`
   244‚Üí2. Import dependencies (logger, config, exceptions)
   245‚Üí3. Implement main functionality
   246‚Üí4. Add rate limiting checks
   247‚Üí5. Add error handling
   248‚Üí6. Write unit tests in `tests/`
   249‚Üí7. Update CLI command in `src/cli/commands/`
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí### Adding a New CLI Command
   252‚Üí
   253‚Üí1. Create command file in `src/cli/commands/`
   254‚Üí2. Use Click decorators
   255‚Üí3. Add to `cli()` group in `src/cli/main.py`
   256‚Üí4. Use Rich for output formatting
   257‚Üí5. Handle errors gracefully
   258‚Üí
   259‚Üí### Updating Selectors
   260‚Üí
   261‚ÜíWhen Maimai UI changes:
   262‚Üí
   263‚Üí1. Use Chrome DevTools (F12)
   264‚Üí2. Inspect target elements
   265‚Üí3. Find stable selectors (avoid dynamic classes)
   266‚Üí4. Update `config/selectors.json`
   267‚Üí5. Test with `playwright codegen maimai.cn`
   268‚Üí6. Update version number in selectors.json
   269‚Üí
   270‚Üí## Next Steps
   271‚Üí
   272‚Üí### Week 1 Actions (Current Phase)
   273‚Üí
   274‚Üí- [x] Project structure created
   275‚Üí- [x] Configuration files set up
   276‚Üí- [x] Database models implemented
   277‚Üí- [x] Core utilities developed
   278‚Üí- [ ] Browser manager implementation
   279‚Üí- [ ] Login flow implementation
   280‚Üí- [ ] Session persistence testing
   281‚Üí
   282‚Üí### Phase 2 Goals (Weeks 3-4)
   283‚Üí
   284‚Üí- Implement search module
   285‚Üí- Implement profile extraction
   286‚Üí- Update selectors with real Maimai data
   287‚Üí- Test anti-detection strategies
   288‚Üí
   289‚ÜíSee [PROPOSAL.md](PROPOSAL.md) for complete implementation roadmap.
   290‚Üí
   291‚Üí## Important Notes
   292‚Üí
   293‚Üí1. **Selectors are placeholders** - Must be updated before use
   294‚Üí2. **Rate limits are hard-coded** - Cannot be overridden
   295‚Üí3. **Human confirmation required** - For all message sending
   296‚Üí4. **Local storage only** - No cloud sync
   297‚Üí5. **Session expires** - Re-login weekly
   298‚Üí6. **Legal compliance** - Review Maimai ToS before use
   299‚Üí
   300‚Üí## Support
   301‚Üí
   302‚ÜíFor questions or issues:
   303‚Üí- Check [README.md](README.md) for usage instructions
   304‚Üí- Review [PROPOSAL.md](PROPOSAL.md) for technical details
   305‚Üí- Open an issue on GitHub
   306‚Üí
   307‚Üí---
   308‚Üí
   309‚Üí**Last Updated**: 2026-01-30
   310‚Üí**Phase**: 1 - Foundation (In Progress)
   311‚Üí**Next Milestone**: Browser automation implementation
   312‚Üí

    58‚Üítestpaths = ["tests"]

1‚Üí"""Core browser automation modules."""
     2‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Browser manager for Playwright automation.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíProvides a singleton browser manager that connects to an existing Chrome
     4‚Üíinstance via CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol). Supports multi-tab management
     5‚Üífor workflows that open new tabs (e.g., enterprise recruitment).
     6‚Üí"""
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üíimport asyncio
     9‚Üífrom pathlib import Path
    10‚Üífrom typing import List, Optional
    11‚Üífrom datetime import datetime
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üífrom patchright.async_api import async_playwright, Browser, BrowserContext, Page, Playwright
    14‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üífrom src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    17‚Üífrom src.core.session import SessionManager, get_session_manager
    18‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import BrowserError
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üíclass BrowserManager:
    22‚Üí    """
    23‚Üí    Singleton browser manager for Maimai automation.
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí    Connects to a user-managed Chrome instance via CDP endpoint.
    26‚Üí    The user starts Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and logs in
    27‚Üí    manually. This manager connects/disconnects without closing Chrome.
    28‚Üí    """
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí    _instance: Optional['BrowserManager'] = None
    31‚Üí    _lock: asyncio.Lock = None  # initialized once at class level
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí    def __new__(cls, **kwargs):
    34‚Üí        if cls._instance is None:
    35‚Üí            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
    36‚Üí            cls._instance._initialized = False
    37‚Üí        return cls._instance
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí    def __init__(
    40‚Üí        self,
    41‚Üí        config: Optional[Config] = None,
    42‚Üí        session_manager: Optional[SessionManager] = None,
    43‚Üí    ):
    44‚Üí        if self._initialized:
    45‚Üí            return
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí        self._playwright: Optional[Playwright] = None
    48‚Üí        self._browser: Optional[Browser] = None
    49‚Üí        self._context: Optional[BrowserContext] = None
    50‚Üí        self._page: Optional[Page] = None
    51‚Üí        self._primary_page: Optional[Page] = None  # Original tab to return to
    52‚Üí        self._opened_tabs: List[Page] = []  # Tabs opened during our session
    53‚Üí        self._session_manager: SessionManager = session_manager or get_session_manager()
    54‚Üí        self._config: Config = config or get_config()
    55‚Üí        self._cdp_endpoint: Optional[str] = None
    56‚Üí        self._owned_page: bool = False  # True if we created the page (vs reusing existing)
    57‚Üí        self._initialized = True
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí    @property
    60‚Üí    def is_running(self) -> bool:
    61‚Üí        """Check if browser is currently connected."""
    62‚Üí        return self._browser is not None and self._browser.is_connected()
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí    @property
    65‚Üí    def page(self) -> Optional[Page]:
    66‚Üí        """Get current page instance."""
    67‚Üí        return self._page
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí    @property
    70‚Üí    def context(self) -> Optional[BrowserContext]:
    71‚Üí        """Get current browser context."""
    72‚Üí        return self._context
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí    @classmethod
    75‚Üí    def _ensure_lock(cls):
    76‚Üí        """Lazily create the asyncio.Lock inside a running event loop.
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí        Uses a double-check pattern: if two coroutines race here, only
    79‚Üí        the first one creates the lock (asyncio is single-threaded within
    80‚Üí        one event loop, so the second check after creation is safe).
    81‚Üí        """
    82‚Üí        if cls._lock is None:
    83‚Üí            cls._lock = asyncio.Lock()
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí    def set_cdp_endpoint(self, endpoint: str) -> None:
    86‚Üí        """Override CDP endpoint (e.g. from CLI --cdp flag)."""
    87‚Üí        self._cdp_endpoint = endpoint
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí    async def start(self) -> Page:
    90‚Üí        """
    91‚Üí        Connect to Chrome via CDP and return a page.
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí        Returns:
    94‚Üí            Configured Playwright page instance
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí        Raises:
    97‚Üí            BrowserError: If connection fails
    98‚Üí        """
    99‚Üí        self._ensure_lock()
   100‚Üí        async with self._lock:
   101‚Üí            if self.is_running:
   102‚Üí                logger.debug("Browser already connected, returning existing page")
   103‚Üí                return self._page
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí            try:
   106‚Üí                # Determine CDP endpoint
   107‚Üí                endpoint = self._cdp_endpoint or self._config.get(
   108‚Üí                    'browser.cdp_endpoint', 'http://localhost:9222'
   109‚Üí                )
   110‚Üí                timeout = self._config.get('browser.timeout', 30000)
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí                logger.info(f"Connecting to Chrome via CDP: {endpoint}")
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí                # Start Playwright
   115‚Üí                self._playwright = await async_playwright().start()
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí                # Connect to existing Chrome via CDP
   118‚Üí                self._browser = await self._playwright.chromium.connect_over_cdp(endpoint)
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí                # Get the default context (the user's browser context with cookies)
   121‚Üí                contexts = self._browser.contexts
   122‚Üí                if contexts:
   123‚Üí                    self._context = contexts[0]
   124‚Üí                else:
   125‚Üí                    # Fallback: create a new context (shouldn't happen with CDP)
   126‚Üí                    logger.warning("No existing context found, creating new one")
   127‚Üí                    self._context = await self._browser.new_context()
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí                # Set default timeout
   130‚Üí                self._context.set_default_timeout(timeout)
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí                # Reuse existing page or create one
   133‚Üí                pages = self._context.pages
   134‚Üí                if pages:
   135‚Üí                    self._page = pages[0]
   136‚Üí                    self._owned_page = False
   137‚Üí                    logger.info(f"Reusing existing tab: {self._page.url}")
   138‚Üí                else:
   139‚Üí                    self._page = await self._context.new_page()
   140‚Üí                    self._owned_page = True
   141‚Üí                    logger.info("Created new tab")
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí                # Remember the primary tab
   144‚Üí                self._primary_page = self._page
   145‚Üí                self._opened_tabs = []
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí                logger.info("Connected to Chrome via CDP successfully")
   148‚Üí                return self._page
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí            except Exception as e:
   151‚Üí                logger.error(f"Failed to connect to Chrome via CDP: {e}")
   152‚Üí                await self._close_internal()
   153‚Üí                raise BrowserError(
   154‚Üí                    f"Failed to connect to Chrome via CDP at {endpoint}. "
   155‚Üí                    f"Make sure Chrome is running with --remote-debugging-port=9222. "
   156‚Üí                    f"Error: {e}"
   157‚Üí                )
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí    async def close(self) -> None:
   160‚Üí        """Disconnect from Chrome via CDP. Does NOT close the user's Chrome."""
   161‚Üí        self._ensure_lock()
   162‚Üí        async with self._lock:
   163‚Üí            await self._close_internal()
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí    async def _close_internal(self) -> None:
   166‚Üí        """Actual cleanup logic ‚Äî callable without acquiring _lock.
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí        Must only be called while the lock is already held, or from
   169‚Üí        start()'s except block (which already holds the lock).
   170‚Üí        """
   171‚Üí        try:
   172‚Üí            # Close tabs we opened during the session
   173‚Üí            for tab in self._opened_tabs:
   174‚Üí                try:
   175‚Üí                    if not tab.is_closed():
   176‚Üí                        await tab.close()
   177‚Üí                except Exception:
   178‚Üí                    pass
   179‚Üí            self._opened_tabs = []
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí            # Only close pages we created, not the user's existing tabs
   182‚Üí            if self._page and self._owned_page:
   183‚Üí                try:
   184‚Üí                    await self._page.close()
   185‚Üí                except Exception:
   186‚Üí                    pass
   187‚Üí            self._page = None
   188‚Üí            self._primary_page = None
   189‚Üí            self._owned_page = False
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí            # Don't close the context ‚Äî it belongs to the user's Chrome
   192‚Üí            self._context = None
   193‚Üí
   194‚Üí            # browser.close() in CDP mode just disconnects, doesn't kill Chrome
   195‚Üí            if self._browser:
   196‚Üí                try:
   197‚Üí                    await self._browser.close()
   198‚Üí                except Exception as e:
   199‚Üí                    logger.error(f"Error closing browser connection: {e}")
   200‚Üí                finally:
   201‚Üí                    self._browser = None
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí            if self._playwright:
   204‚Üí                try:
   205‚Üí                    await self._playwright.stop()
   206‚Üí                except Exception as e:
   207‚Üí                    logger.error(f"Error stopping Playwright: {e}")
   208‚Üí                finally:
   209‚Üí                    self._playwright = None
   210‚Üí
   211‚Üí            logger.info("Disconnected from Chrome (browser stays open)")
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üí        except Exception as e:
   214‚Üí            logger.error(f"Error disconnecting from Chrome: {e}")
   215‚Üí
   216‚Üí    async def wait_for_new_tab(self, timeout: int = 10000) -> Page:
   217‚Üí        """Wait for a click-triggered new tab, apply stealth, and switch to it.
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí        Call this right after performing an action that opens a new tab
   220‚Üí        (e.g., clicking a link with target=_blank).
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí        Args:
   223‚Üí            timeout: Max wait time in milliseconds
   224‚Üí
   225‚Üí        Returns:
   226‚Üí            The new Page instance (also set as the active page)
   227‚Üí
   228‚Üí        Raises:
   229‚Üí            BrowserError: If no new tab appears within timeout
   230‚Üí        """
   231‚Üí        if not self._context:
   232‚Üí            raise BrowserError("No active context for wait_for_new_tab")
   233‚Üí
   234‚Üí        try:
   235‚Üí            new_page = await self._context.wait_for_event(
   236‚Üí                "page", timeout=timeout
   237‚Üí            )
   238‚Üí            await new_page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded")
   239‚Üí            self._opened_tabs.append(new_page)
   240‚Üí            self._page = new_page
   241‚Üí            logger.info(f"New tab captured: {new_page.url}")
   242‚Üí            return new_page
   243‚Üí        except Exception as e:
   244‚Üí            raise BrowserError(f"No new tab opened within {timeout}ms: {e}")
   245‚Üí
   246‚Üí    def switch_to_primary_tab(self) -> Page:
   247‚Üí        """Switch back to the original (primary) tab.
   248‚Üí
   249‚Üí        Returns:
   250‚Üí            The primary Page instance
   251‚Üí        """
   252‚Üí        if not self._primary_page or self._primary_page.is_closed():
   253‚Üí            raise BrowserError("Primary tab is not available")
   254‚Üí
   255‚Üí        self._page = self._primary_page
   256‚Üí        logger.debug(f"Switched to primary tab: {self._page.url}")
   257‚Üí        return self._page
   258‚Üí
   259‚Üí    async def close_current_tab(self) -> Page:
   260‚Üí        """Close the current active tab and switch to the previous one.
   261‚Üí
   262‚Üí        Will not close the primary tab. After closing, switches to the
   263‚Üí        most recent remaining tab or falls back to the primary tab.
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí        Returns:
   266‚Üí            The page that became active after closing
   267‚Üí
   268‚Üí        Raises:
   269‚Üí            BrowserError: If trying to close the primary tab
   270‚Üí        """
   271‚Üí        if not self._page:
   272‚Üí            raise BrowserError("No active page to close")
   273‚Üí
   274‚Üí        if self._page == self._primary_page:
   275‚Üí            raise BrowserError("Cannot close the primary tab")
   276‚Üí
   277‚Üí        closing = self._page
   278‚Üí        try:
   279‚Üí            # Remove from opened_tabs tracking
   280‚Üí            if closing in self._opened_tabs:
   281‚Üí                self._opened_tabs.remove(closing)
   282‚Üí            await closing.close()
   283‚Üí        except Exception as e:
   284‚Üí            logger.warning(f"Error closing tab: {e}")
   285‚Üí
   286‚Üí        # Switch to previous tab
   287‚Üí        if self._context and self._context.pages:
   288‚Üí            self._page = self._context.pages[-1]
   289‚Üí        else:
   290‚Üí            self._page = self._primary_page
   291‚Üí
   292‚Üí        logger.debug(f"Closed tab, switched to: {self._page.url if self._page else 'none'}")
   293‚Üí        return self._page
   294‚Üí
   295‚Üí    async def save_session(self) -> None:
   296‚Üí        """Save current session to disk (backup of cookies)."""
   297‚Üí        if self._context:
   298‚Üí            await self._session_manager.save(self._context)
   299‚Üí        else:
   300‚Üí            logger.warning("No active context to save session from")
   301‚Üí
   302‚Üí    async def screenshot(self, name: str = None) -> str:
   303‚Üí        """
   304‚Üí        Take a screenshot of current page.
   305‚Üí
   306‚Üí        Args:
   307‚Üí            name: Screenshot name (timestamp used if None)
   308‚Üí
   309‚Üí        Returns:
   310‚Üí            Path to saved screenshot
   311‚Üí        """
   312‚Üí        if not self._page:
   313‚Üí            raise BrowserError("No active page for screenshot")
   314‚Üí
   315‚Üí        screenshot_dir = Path(self._config.get('data.screenshot_dir', 'data/screenshots'))
   316‚Üí        screenshot_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   317‚Üí
   318‚Üí        if name is None:
   319‚Üí            name = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')
   320‚Üí
   321‚Üí        filepath = screenshot_dir / f"{name}.png"
   322‚Üí
   323‚Üí        await self._page.screenshot(path=str(filepath), full_page=True)
   324‚Üí        logger.info(f"Screenshot saved: {filepath}")
   325‚Üí
   326‚Üí        return str(filepath)
   327‚Üí
   328‚Üí
   329‚Üí
   330‚Üí# Global browser manager instance
   331‚Üí_browser_manager: Optional[BrowserManager] = None
   332‚Üí
   333‚Üí
   334‚Üídef get_browser_manager() -> BrowserManager:
   335‚Üí    """Get global browser manager instance."""
   336‚Üí    global _browser_manager
   337‚Üí    if _browser_manager is None:
   338‚Üí        _browser_manager = BrowserManager()
   339‚Üí    return _browser_manager
   340‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Session management for persistent browser sessions.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíHandles saving and loading browser sessions (cookies, localStorage)
     4‚Üíto enable resuming without re-login.
     5‚Üí"""
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üíimport json
     8‚Üíimport os
     9‚Üíimport stat
    10‚Üíimport tempfile
    11‚Üífrom pathlib import Path
    12‚Üífrom datetime import datetime, timezone
    13‚Üífrom typing import Optional
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üífrom cryptography.fernet import Fernet
    16‚Üífrom patchright.async_api import BrowserContext
    17‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üífrom src.utils.config import get_config
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üíclass SessionManager:
    23‚Üí    """Manages browser session persistence."""
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí    def __init__(self, session_path: Optional[str] = None):
    26‚Üí        """
    27‚Üí        Initialize session manager.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí        Args:
    30‚Üí            session_path: Path to session file. Uses config default if None.
    31‚Üí        """
    32‚Üí        if session_path is None:
    33‚Üí            config = get_config()
    34‚Üí            session_path = config.get('data.session_path', 'data/.session/maimai_session.json')
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí        self.session_path = Path(session_path)
    37‚Üí        self._key_path = self.session_path.parent / '.session_key'
    38‚Üí        self._fernet: Optional[Fernet] = None
    39‚Üí        self._ensure_session_dir()
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí    def _ensure_session_dir(self) -> None:
    42‚Üí        """Ensure session directory exists with restrictive permissions."""
    43‚Üí        session_dir = self.session_path.parent
    44‚Üí        session_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    45‚Üí        # Restrict directory to owner only (rwx------)
    46‚Üí        try:
    47‚Üí            os.chmod(session_dir, stat.S_IRWXU)
    48‚Üí        except OSError:
    49‚Üí            logger.warning("Could not set restrictive permissions on session directory")
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí    def _get_fernet(self) -> Fernet:
    52‚Üí        """Get or create Fernet encryption instance."""
    53‚Üí        if self._fernet is not None:
    54‚Üí            return self._fernet
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí        if self._key_path.exists():
    57‚Üí            key = self._key_path.read_bytes().strip()
    58‚Üí        else:
    59‚Üí            key = Fernet.generate_key()
    60‚Üí            # Write key with owner-only permissions
    61‚Üí            fd = os.open(str(self._key_path), os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC, 0o600)
    62‚Üí            try:
    63‚Üí                os.write(fd, key)
    64‚Üí            finally:
    65‚Üí                os.close(fd)
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí        self._fernet = Fernet(key)
    68‚Üí        return self._fernet
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí    def _decrypt_session_data(self) -> dict:
    71‚Üí        """Decrypt and parse the stored session file.
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí        Returns:
    74‚Üí            Parsed session data dict.
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí        Raises:
    77‚Üí            Exception: If decryption or JSON parsing fails.
    78‚Üí        """
    79‚Üí        encrypted = self.session_path.read_bytes()
    80‚Üí        plaintext = self._get_fernet().decrypt(encrypted)
    81‚Üí        return json.loads(plaintext.decode('utf-8'))
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí    def _write_secure(self, path: Path, data: bytes) -> None:
    84‚Üí        """Atomically write data to file with owner-only permissions (0600).
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí        Writes to a temp file first, then renames to target path so that
    87‚Üí        a crash mid-write never corrupts the existing file.
    88‚Üí        """
    89‚Üí        tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=str(path.parent), suffix=".tmp")
    90‚Üí        try:
    91‚Üí            os.write(tmp_fd, data)
    92‚Üí            os.close(tmp_fd)
    93‚Üí            tmp_fd = -1  # mark as closed
    94‚Üí            os.chmod(tmp_path, 0o600)
    95‚Üí            os.replace(tmp_path, str(path))
    96‚Üí        except BaseException:
    97‚Üí            if tmp_fd >= 0:
    98‚Üí                os.close(tmp_fd)
    99‚Üí            try:
   100‚Üí                os.unlink(tmp_path)
   101‚Üí            except OSError:
   102‚Üí                pass
   103‚Üí            raise
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí    def has_saved_session(self) -> bool:
   106‚Üí        """
   107‚Üí        Check if a saved session exists.
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí        Returns:
   110‚Üí            True if session file exists
   111‚Üí        """
   112‚Üí        return self.session_path.exists()
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí    async def save(self, context: BrowserContext) -> None:
   115‚Üí        """
   116‚Üí        Save browser session state.
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí        Args:
   119‚Üí            context: Playwright browser context
   120‚Üí        """
   121‚Üí        try:
   122‚Üí            # Get storage state (cookies + localStorage)
   123‚Üí            storage_state = await context.storage_state()
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí            # Add metadata
   126‚Üí            session_data = {
   127‚Üí                'saved_at': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   128‚Üí                'storage_state': storage_state
   129‚Üí            }
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí            # Encrypt and write with restrictive permissions
   132‚Üí            plaintext = json.dumps(session_data, ensure_ascii=False).encode('utf-8')
   133‚Üí            encrypted = self._get_fernet().encrypt(plaintext)
   134‚Üí            self._write_secure(self.session_path, encrypted)
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí            logger.info(f"Session saved (encrypted) to {self.session_path}")
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üí        except Exception as e:
   139‚Üí            logger.error(f"Failed to save session: {e}")
   140‚Üí            raise
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí    async def clear(self) -> None:
   143‚Üí        """Clear saved session and encryption key."""
   144‚Üí        if self.session_path.exists():
   145‚Üí            self.session_path.unlink()
   146‚Üí            logger.info("Session cleared")
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üí        # Also clear storage state file if exists
   149‚Üí        state_path = self.session_path.parent / 'storage_state.json'
   150‚Üí        if state_path.exists():
   151‚Üí            state_path.unlink()
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üí        # Remove encryption key so a fresh one is generated next time
   154‚Üí        if self._key_path.exists():
   155‚Üí            self._key_path.unlink()
   156‚Üí        self._fernet = None
   157‚Üí
   158‚Üí    def get_session_info(self) -> Optional[dict]:
   159‚Üí        """
   160‚Üí        Get information about saved session.
   161‚Üí
   162‚Üí        Returns:
   163‚Üí            Dict with session metadata or None if no session
   164‚Üí        """
   165‚Üí        if not self.has_saved_session():
   166‚Üí            return None
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí        try:
   169‚Üí            session_data = self._decrypt_session_data()
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üí            saved_at = session_data.get('saved_at')
   172‚Üí            storage_state = session_data.get('storage_state', {})
   173‚Üí            cookies = storage_state.get('cookies', [])
   174‚Üí
   175‚Üí            # Calculate age
   176‚Üí            if saved_at:
   177‚Üí                saved_datetime = datetime.fromisoformat(saved_at)
   178‚Üí                if saved_datetime.tzinfo is None:
   179‚Üí                    saved_datetime = saved_datetime.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   180‚Üí                age = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - saved_datetime
   181‚Üí                age_str = f"{age.days}d {age.seconds // 3600}h"
   182‚Üí            else:
   183‚Üí                age_str = "unknown"
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üí            return {
   186‚Üí                'saved_at': saved_at,
   187‚Üí                'age': age_str,
   188‚Üí                'cookie_count': len(cookies),
   189‚Üí            }
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí        except Exception as e:
   192‚Üí            logger.error(f"Failed to get session info: {e}")
   193‚Üí            return None
   194‚Üí
   195‚Üí
   196‚Üí# Global session manager instance
   197‚Üí_session_manager = None
   198‚Üí
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üídef get_session_manager() -> SessionManager:
   201‚Üí    """Get global session manager instance."""
   202‚Üí    global _session_manager
   203‚Üí    if _session_manager is None:
   204‚Üí        _session_manager = SessionManager()
   205‚Üí    return _session_manager
   206‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Anti-detection module for browser automation.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíImplements human-like interaction via Emunium + Patchright:
     4‚Üí- Human-like scrolling behavior (chunked, variable speed)
     5‚Üí- Human-like clicking via Emunium (natural mouse movement)
     6‚Üí- Human-like typing via Emunium (variable keystroke timing)
     7‚Üí
     8‚ÜíNote: Webdriver property hiding and navigator masking are handled
     9‚Üíby Patchright automatically in CDP mode.
    10‚Üí"""
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üíimport random
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üífrom patchright.async_api import Page
    15‚Üífrom emunium import EmuniumPlaywright
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import SelectorNotFoundError
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üíasync def human_scroll(page: Page, direction: str = 'down', distance: int = None) -> None:
    21‚Üí    """
    22‚Üí    Perform human-like scrolling with variable speed.
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí    Args:
    25‚Üí        page: Playwright page instance
    26‚Üí        direction: 'up' or 'down'
    27‚Üí        distance: Scroll distance in pixels (random if None)
    28‚Üí    """
    29‚Üí    if direction not in ('up', 'down'):
    30‚Üí        raise ValueError(f"Invalid scroll direction: {direction!r}, expected 'up' or 'down'")
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí    if distance is None:
    33‚Üí        distance = random.randint(200, 600)
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí    if direction == 'up':
    36‚Üí        distance = -distance
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí    # Break scroll into smaller chunks
    39‚Üí    chunks = random.randint(3, 8)
    40‚Üí    chunk_size = distance // chunks
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí    for i in range(chunks):
    43‚Üí        # Add variance to each chunk
    44‚Üí        actual_chunk = chunk_size + random.randint(-20, 20)
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí        await page.mouse.wheel(0, actual_chunk)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí        # Variable delay between scroll chunks
    49‚Üí        delay = random.uniform(0.05, 0.15)
    50‚Üí        await page.wait_for_timeout(delay * 1000)
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí    # Small pause after scrolling (human would look at content)
    53‚Üí    await page.wait_for_timeout(random.uniform(0.2, 0.5) * 1000)
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üíasync def human_click(page: Page, selector: str) -> None:
    57‚Üí    """
    58‚Üí    Click an element with human-like behavior using Emunium.
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí    Emunium provides natural mouse movement and click simulation.
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí    Args:
    63‚Üí        page: Playwright page instance
    64‚Üí        selector: CSS selector for element to click
    65‚Üí    """
    66‚Üí    try:
    67‚Üí        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    68‚Üí    except Exception as e:
    69‚Üí        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    70‚Üí            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    71‚Üí        raise
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí    if element is None:
    74‚Üí        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
    77‚Üí    await emunium.click_at(element)
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üíasync def human_type(page: Page, selector: str, text: str) -> None:
    81‚Üí    """
    82‚Üí    Type text with human-like timing using Emunium.
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí    Args:
    85‚Üí        page: Playwright page instance
    86‚Üí        selector: CSS selector for input element
    87‚Üí        text: Text to type
    88‚Üí    """
    89‚Üí    try:
    90‚Üí        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    91‚Üí    except Exception as e:
    92‚Üí        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    93‚Üí            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    94‚Üí        raise
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí    if element is None:
    97‚Üí        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    98‚Üí
    99‚Üí    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
   100‚Üí    await emunium.type_at(element, text)
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Feature modules for Maimai Assistant."""
     2‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Authentication module for Maimai login.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíIn CDP mode, the user logs in manually in their own Chrome before running
     4‚ÜíCLI commands. This module verifies login status and provides guidance
     5‚Üíif the user is not logged in.
     6‚Üí"""
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üíimport asyncio
     9‚Üífrom typing import Optional
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üífrom patchright.async_api import Page
    12‚Üífrom loguru import logger
    13‚Üífrom rich.console import Console
    14‚Üífrom rich.panel import Panel
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üífrom src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17‚Üífrom src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    18‚Üífrom src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üíconsole = Console()
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üíasync def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    24‚Üí    """
    25‚Üí    Verify login status in CDP-connected Chrome.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí    In CDP mode, the user is expected to already be logged in.
    28‚Üí    If not, instructions are printed for manual login in the browser.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí    Args:
    31‚Üí        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí    Returns:
    34‚Üí        True if logged in
    35‚Üí    """
    36‚Üí    if browser is None:
    37‚Üí        browser = get_browser_manager()
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí    # Connect to Chrome via CDP
    40‚Üí    page = await browser.start()
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí    # Check if already logged in
    43‚Üí    if await check_login_status(page):
    44‚Üí        console.print("[green]Already logged in![/green]")
    45‚Üí        # Save session as backup
    46‚Üí        await browser.save_session()
    47‚Üí        return True
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí    # Not logged in ‚Äî guide the user
    50‚Üí    console.print(Panel(
    51‚Üí        "[bold]Not Logged In[/bold]\n\n"
    52‚Üí        "Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser:\n"
    53‚Üí        "1. Go to https://maimai.cn/login in Chrome\n"
    54‚Üí        "2. Scan QR code with Maimai app, OR\n"
    55‚Üí        "3. Enter phone number and verification code\n\n"
    56‚Üí        "[dim]Then run this command again to verify.[/dim]",
    57‚Üí        title="Maimai Login",
    58‚Üí        border_style="cyan"
    59‚Üí    ))
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí    # Poll for login (user may log in while we wait)
    62‚Üí    max_wait_seconds = 300  # 5 minutes timeout
    63‚Üí    poll_interval = 3
    64‚Üí    elapsed = 0
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí    console.print("[dim]Waiting for login... (timeout: 5 minutes)[/dim]")
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí    while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
    69‚Üí        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
    70‚Üí        elapsed += poll_interval
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí        # Check for captcha
    73‚Üí        await check_captcha(page)
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí        if await check_login_status(page):
    76‚Üí            console.print("[green]Login successful![/green]")
    77‚Üí            await browser.save_session()
    78‚Üí            console.print("[green]Session saved as backup[/green]")
    79‚Üí            return True
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí    console.print("[red]Login timeout. Please log in manually and try again.[/red]")
    82‚Üí    return False
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üíasync def check_login_status(page: Page) -> bool:
    86‚Üí    """
    87‚Üí    Check if user is currently logged in by navigating to a protected page
    88‚Üí    and observing whether Maimai redirects to the login page.
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí    API probes can give false positives (server returns 200 but session is
    91‚Üí    actually stale). The redirect-based approach is reliable because Maimai's
    92‚Üí    server-side auth check only fires on real page navigations, and it will
    93‚Üí    redirect to the login page if the session is invalid.
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí    Args:
    96‚Üí        page: Playwright page instance
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí    Returns:
    99‚Üí        True if logged in (no redirect to login page)
   100‚Üí    """
   101‚Üí    try:
   102‚Üí        # Navigate to a protected page that requires authentication.
   103‚Üí        # The search center page reliably triggers server-side auth checks.
   104‚Üí        protected_url = "https://maimai.cn/web/search_center"
   105‚Üí        await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí        # Wait for any redirects to settle (server-side auth redirect happens
   108‚Üí        # after initial page load and JS execution)
   109‚Üí        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí        # Check 1: URL-based detection - did we get redirected to login?
   112‚Üí        final_url = page.url
   113‚Üí        if 'login' in final_url.lower():
   114‚Üí            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=redirected_to_login (url={final_url})")
   115‚Üí            return False
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí        # Check 2: Page content detection - look for login form elements
   118‚Üí        # even if URL didn't change (some login overlays don't change the URL)
   119‚Üí        is_login_page = await page.evaluate("""
   120‚Üí            () => {
   121‚Üí                const bodyText = document.body ? document.body.innerText : '';
   122‚Üí                const loginIndicators = [
   123‚Üí                    'Ê¨¢Ëøé‰ΩøÁî®ËÑâËÑâ',
   124‚Üí                    'ÁôªÂΩï/Ê≥®ÂÜå',
   125‚Üí                    'ËØ∑ËæìÂÖ•ÊâãÊú∫Âè∑',
   126‚Üí                    'Ëé∑ÂèñÈ™åËØÅÁÝÅ',
   127‚Üí                    'Êâ´ÁÝÅÁôªÂΩï',
   128‚Üí                    'ÊâãÊú∫Âè∑ÁôªÂΩï'
   129‚Üí                ];
   130‚Üí                for (const indicator of loginIndicators) {
   131‚Üí                    if (bodyText.includes(indicator)) {
   132‚Üí                        return { is_login: true, matched: indicator };
   133‚Üí                    }
   134‚Üí                }
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí                // Also check for login form elements in DOM
   137‚Üí                const loginForm = document.querySelector(
   138‚Üí                    'input[type="tel"], input[placeholder*="ÊâãÊú∫"], ' +
   139‚Üí                    '[class*="qrcode"], [class*="scan-login"]'
   140‚Üí                );
   141‚Üí                if (loginForm) {
   142‚Üí                    return { is_login: true, matched: 'login_form_element' };
   143‚Üí                }
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí                return { is_login: false, matched: null };
   146‚Üí            }
   147‚Üí        """)
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí        if is_login_page.get('is_login', False):
   150‚Üí            detail = is_login_page.get('matched', 'unknown')
   151‚Üí            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=login_page_detected ({detail})")
   152‚Üí            return False
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí        logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=True, detail=protected_page_accessible (url={final_url})")
   155‚Üí        return True
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí    except Exception as e:
   158‚Üí        logger.error(f"Error checking login status: {e}")
   159‚Üí        return False
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí
   162‚Üíasync def ensure_logged_in(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
   163‚Üí    """
   164‚Üí    Ensure user is logged in, prompting for login if needed.
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí    Args:
   167‚Üí        browser: Browser manager instance
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí    Returns:
   170‚Üí        True if logged in (existing or new login)
   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí    Raises:
   173‚Üí        SessionExpiredError: If login fails
   174‚Üí    """
   175‚Üí    if browser is None:
   176‚Üí        browser = get_browser_manager()
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí    # Connect to Chrome via CDP if not already connected
   179‚Üí    if not browser.is_running:
   180‚Üí        await browser.start()
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí    # Check login status
   183‚Üí    if browser.page:
   184‚Üí        if await check_login_status(browser.page):
   185‚Üí            return True
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí    # Not logged in ‚Äî guide user
   188‚Üí    console.print("[yellow]Not logged in. Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser.[/yellow]")
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí    if await login(browser):
   191‚Üí        return True
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí    raise SessionExpiredError("Not logged in to Maimai. Please log in manually in Chrome and try again.")
   194‚Üí

1‚Üí"""Search module for finding candidates on Maimai.
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíHandles candidate search with filters, pagination,
     4‚Üíand extraction of basic candidate information.
     5‚Üí"""

    32‚Üíconsole = Console()
    33‚Üí

    36‚ÜíMAIMAI_SEARCH_URL = "https://maimai.cn/web/search_center"
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí# Names indicating paid/premium-only profiles that should be skipped
    39‚ÜíPREMIUM_PLACEHOLDER_NAMES = {"ËÑâËÑâÈ´ò‰ª∑ÂÄº‰∫∫ËÑâ"}
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí# Keywords indicating non-R&D roles (to be filtered out)
    42‚ÜíNON_RD_KEYWORDS = {
    43‚Üí    "ÈîÄÂîÆ", "ËøêËê•", "‰∫ßÂìÅ", "Â∏ÇÂú∫", "ÁîµÂ≠êÂïÜÂä°", "ÁîµÂïÜ",
    44‚Üí    "ÂïÜÂä°", "ÂìÅÁâå", "ÂÖ¨ÂÖ≥", "ÂÆ¢Êúç", "ÈááË¥≠", "ÊãõÂïÜ",
    45‚Üí    "‰∫∫Âäõ", "‰∫∫‰∫ã", "HR", "hr", "HRBP", "Ë°åÊîø",
    46‚Üí    "Ë¥¢Âä°", "‰ºöËÆ°", "ÂÆ°ËÆ°", "Ê≥ïÂä°", "Ê≥ïÂæã", "ÂêàËßÑ",
    47‚Üí    "ÂÜÖÂÆπ", "ÁºñËæë", "ÁøªËØë", "ÊïôËÇ≤", "ÂüπËÆ≠", "ÊïôÂ≠¶",
    48‚Üí    "Áâ©ÊµÅ", "‰æõÂ∫îÈìæ", "‰ªìÂÇ®",
    49‚Üí}

    53‚Üídef _is_non_rd_title(title: str) -> bool:
    54‚Üí    """Check if a title indicates a non-R&D role.
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí    Strict mode: if any non-R&D keyword appears, filter out regardless of
    57‚Üí    whether R&D keywords are also present (e.g. "ÊäÄÊúØ‰∫ßÂìÅÁªèÁêÜ" is filtered).
    58‚Üí    Returns False (keep) only if title is empty or contains no non-R&D keywords.
    59‚Üí    """
    60‚Üí    if not title:
    61‚Üí        return False
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí    # Strict: any non-R&D keyword present ‚Üí filter out
    64‚Üí    if any(kw in title for kw in NON_RD_KEYWORDS):
    65‚Üí        return True
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí    return False
